title: Revise existing schemas for recommendation alignment
description:
Phase 1 of schema-update epic. All changes in schemas/ directory.

Changes:
(1) Add search_orientation:float and attentional_bias:float to LatentDeviation in schemas/persona.py. search_orientation makes Payne Index tendency a sampled latent trait (not just emergent output). attentional_bias creates controllable divergence between dwell attention weights and choice preference weights — the critical independence property per mouselab.md recommendation.
(2) Add EventType enum (CELL_HOVER, CELL_OPEN, COLUMN_ADD, SORT_APPLY, CHOICE) and event_type:EventType field to AcquisitionEvent in schemas/trace.py — realistic instrumented-hover framing per mouselab.md.
(3) Add month:int field directly to PersonaConfig (default 0). No wrapper class — persona_sampler creates one PersonaConfig per month with the same base config but month-specific drift state. This keeps the schema simple and avoids class explosion.
(4) Enrich TransactionRecord with order-line fields: sku:str (format: SKU-{category}-{tier}-{seq}), qty:int (1-3, weighted by category), unit_price:float (absolute, derived from price_paid_normalised * base_price), discount_applied:Optional[float] (0.0-0.3, probability proportional to price_sensitivity), payment_method:PaymentMethod (enum: CREDIT_CARD, DEBIT_CARD, PAYPAL, CASH, BNPL). These fields are committed — Phase 2c depends on them.

Acceptance criteria:
- LatentDeviation dataclass has exactly 7 fields (current 5 + search_orientation + attentional_bias)
- EventType enum has 5 members in schemas/trace.py
- PaymentMethod enum has 5 members in schemas/transaction.py
- AcquisitionEvent has event_type:EventType field
- TransactionRecord has sku, qty, unit_price, discount_applied, payment_method fields
- PersonaConfig has month:int field (default 0)
- schemas/__init__.py exports EventType, PaymentMethod and any new types
- All existing tests pass (uv run pytest)
- ruff check + format clean

Downstream impact: blocks all Phase 2 beads. All generators and encoders import from these schemas.
Recommended model: Sonnet. Complexity: Medium.
acceptance_criteria: Update .claude/context/data-contracts.md to reflect new LatentDeviation fields (7 total), EventType enum, PaymentMethod enum, enriched TransactionRecord fields, and PersonaConfig month field.
close_reason: Closed

title: Create campaign generator + schema
description:
New modality: campaign interaction log per docs/modalities.md recommendation #5. Create schema and generator from scratch.

Schema (schemas/campaign.py):
- CampaignEvent: customer_id:str, campaign_id:str, sent_ts:datetime, campaign_type:CampaignType (PROMOTION, NEWSLETTER, RE_ENGAGEMENT, LOYALTY, NEW_PRODUCT), discount_pct:float (0.0-0.5), category:str, opened:bool, clicked:bool, converted:bool, unsub:bool
- CampaignSchedule: defines campaign dispatch rules (frequency, targeting criteria, discount depth by type)

Generator (generator/campaign_generator.py):
- Campaign dispatch: ~4-8 campaigns per month per customer, type distribution weighted by seasonality and customer segment. Discount_pct drawn from campaign_type-dependent distribution (PROMOTION: 0.1-0.3, LOYALTY: 0.05-0.15, others: 0.0).

- Response model with pinned base rates per campaign type:
  PROMOTION:    base_open=0.30, base_click=0.10, base_convert=0.03
  NEWSLETTER:   base_open=0.25, base_click=0.05, base_convert=0.01
  RE_ENGAGEMENT: base_open=0.20, base_click=0.08, base_convert=0.02
  LOYALTY:      base_open=0.40, base_click=0.08, base_convert=0.02
  NEW_PRODUCT:  base_open=0.25, base_click=0.12, base_convert=0.02

- z conditioning via sigmoid(logit(base_rate) + beta * z_relevant), beta=0.5:
  opened: z_relevant = brand_lean (for LOYALTY/NEWSLETTER), price_lean (for PROMOTION)
  clicked: z_relevant = openness (for NEW_PRODUCT), brand_lean (for LOYALTY)
  converted: z_relevant = price_lean (for PROMOTION — discount response), brand_lean (for LOYALTY)

- Funnel: sent -> opened (20-40% across types) -> clicked (5-15% of opened) -> converted (1-5% of clicked) -> unsub (0.5-3% per campaign, cumulative). Each stage conditionally dependent on previous — only evaluate clicked if opened=True, etc.

- Unsub feedback: once unsub=True, no further campaigns sent to that customer.

Acceptance criteria:
- schemas/campaign.py exports CampaignEvent, CampaignType, CampaignSchedule
- Generator outputs to data/synthetic/campaigns/ (campaigns.jsonl)
- Open rate in 20-40% range across full population
- Click rate 5-15% of opened
- Unsub rate <5% cumulative over 12 months
- Response rates vary by archetype (price_lex higher conversion on PROMOTION, brand_affect higher open on LOYALTY)
- No campaigns sent to unsubscribed customers (verify)
- All records have month field for temporal partitioning
- ruff check + format clean

Depends on: Phase 1 (schemas). Downstream: blocks pipeline, campaign encoder.
Recommended model: Sonnet. Complexity: Medium.
close_reason: Closed

title: Create clickstream generator + schema
description:
New modality: web clickstream event log per docs/modalities.md recommendation #2. Create schema and generator from scratch.

Schema (schemas/clickstream.py):
- ClickstreamEvent: customer_id:str, session_id:str, event_ts:datetime, event_type:ClickstreamEventType (PAGE_VIEW, PRODUCT_VIEW, ADD_TO_CART, REMOVE_FROM_CART, SEARCH, FILTER_APPLY, CHECKOUT_START, PURCHASE), page_type:PageType (HOME, CATEGORY, PRODUCT, SEARCH_RESULTS, CART, CHECKOUT), sku_viewed:Optional[str], referrer:ReferrerType (DIRECT, ORGANIC, PAID_SEARCH, EMAIL, SOCIAL), device:DeviceType (DESKTOP, MOBILE, TABLET), dwell_ms:float
- SessionSummary: customer_id, session_id, n_events, session_duration_s, intent:SessionIntent (BROWSE, RESEARCH, BUY), device

Generator (generator/clickstream_generator.py):
- Markov session model with 8 states = event_types. Legal transitions by session intent:

  BROWSE intent (50% of sessions):
    HOME -> CATEGORY (0.6) | exit (0.4)
    CATEGORY -> PRODUCT (0.5) | CATEGORY (0.3) | HOME (0.2)
    PRODUCT -> CATEGORY (0.6) | PRODUCT (0.3) | exit (0.1)

  RESEARCH intent (30% of sessions):
    HOME -> SEARCH (0.8) | CATEGORY (0.2)
    SEARCH -> SEARCH_RESULTS (1.0)
    SEARCH_RESULTS -> PRODUCT (0.7) | SEARCH_RESULTS (0.2) | exit (0.1)
    PRODUCT -> PRODUCT (0.4) | SEARCH_RESULTS (0.4) | CATEGORY (0.2)

  BUY intent (20% of sessions):
    any -> ADD_TO_CART -> CHECKOUT_START -> PURCHASE (with probability 0.7)
    ADD_TO_CART -> REMOVE_FROM_CART (0.15, cart abandonment branch)

- z conditioning: brand_lean increases PRODUCT->PRODUCT transition probability by 0.2 * brand_lean. impulsivity increases exit/bounce probability by 0.15 * impulsivity. involvement scales session length: base_n_events = 3 + 5 * involvement_score.

- Realistic noise: 30-60% sessions are short bounces (1-3 events, no purchase). 10-15% of sessions dropped to customer_id='anonymous' (identity resolution failure) — excluded from encoder training but present in raw output. Dwell times: log-normal with sigma=0.8. Sessions per month: Poisson(lambda=3 + 4 * involvement_score).

Acceptance criteria:
- schemas/clickstream.py exports ClickstreamEvent, ClickstreamEventType, SessionSummary
- Generator outputs to data/synthetic/clickstream/ (session_events.jsonl, sessions.jsonl)
- Session length distribution: median 3-8 events, 30-60% sessions have <=3 events
- Anonymous session fraction: 10-15% (verify in output)
- Dwell time distribution is log-normal (verify via KS test)
- 8 event types present in output
- BUY-intent sessions contain ADD_TO_CART and CHECKOUT_START events
- All output records have month field for temporal partitioning
- ruff check + format clean

Depends on: Phase 1 (schemas). Downstream: blocks pipeline, clickstream encoder.
Recommended model: Sonnet. Complexity: Large.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: michaelarutyunov
comments.text:
CLARITY REVIEW
ISSUE: Markov transition matrix is described conceptually ('transition matrix conditioned on z', 'varies by intent') but the actual state space and transition structure are not defined. The implementer must invent the full transition graph — states, edges, and how z modifies transition probabilities.
QUESTION: What are the Markov model states? What transitions are legal? How does z condition the transition probabilities?
SUGGESTED FIX: Define the state graph explicitly. States = event_types (8 states). Legal transitions by intent:
  BROWSE: HOME→CATEGORY→PRODUCT→CATEGORY or HOME (high dwell on CATEGORY)
  RESEARCH: SEARCH→SEARCH_RESULTS→PRODUCT→PRODUCT (repeat product views)
  BUY: any→ADD_TO_CART→CHECKOUT_START→PURCHASE
z conditioning: brand_lean increases PRODUCT→PRODUCT transitions; impulsivity increases bounce (any→end); involvement increases session length (1-lambda_end). Pin the base transition matrices for each intent.

title: Update pipeline: 12-month temporal structure + partial coverage
description:
Extend generator/pipeline.py to produce temporally-structured data over 12 months with configurable partial modality coverage.

Changes:
(1) Add temporal loop: outer loop over months 1-12. Each month, generate events using the month-specific drift state from persona_sampler temporal trajectory. Event modalities (transactions, clickstream, campaigns) generated per-month. Snapshot modalities (traces, surveys) generated at specific fielding dates.
(2) Implement partial coverage: process traces on configurable subset (default 250 of 1000 customers). Select subset via stratified sampling (equal archetype representation). Remaining 750 customers have traces modality missing. This creates natural missingness for masked-fusion stress test — no artificial dropout needed.
(3) Temporal fielding schedule: traces fielded months 1-2 (on the 250 subset), surveys at months 1 and 7, campaigns throughout months 1-12, clickstream throughout months 1-12, transactions throughout months 1-12.
(4) Output structure: month-partitioned files (e.g., data/synthetic/transactions_month_01.jsonl through _month_12.jsonl) plus merged aggregate files. Add month:int field to all output records.
(5) Drift cohort labeling: mark the 10-15% regime-shift participants with a drift_label field and drift_month field for downstream evaluation.

Acceptance criteria:
- Pipeline generates data for 12 months (configurable via --n-months flag)
- Output files are month-partitioned with month field in every record
- Process traces generated for only the designated subset (default 250)
- Non-trace participants have no trace output (natural missingness)
- Drift cohort is labeled with drift_label and drift_month
- Temporal fielding schedule enforced (traces only months 1-2, surveys months 1 and 7)
- All existing tests pass (updated for temporal structure)
- ruff check + format clean

Depends on: Phase 2a (persona_sampler), Phase 2b (trace_simulator), Phase 2c (transaction_simulator), Phase 2e (clickstream), Phase 2f (campaign). Downstream: blocks temporal split, all encoder training.
Recommended model: Sonnet. Complexity: Medium.

title: Update transaction_simulator: order-line schema + hazard-model timing
description:
Enrich transaction generator with realistic order-line fields and hazard-model inter-purchase timing.

Changes:
(1) Populate new TransactionRecord fields added in Phase 1: sku (format: SKU-{category}-{tier}-{seq}), qty (1-3, weighted by category), unit_price (absolute, derived from price_paid_normalised * base_price), discount_applied (Optional[float], 0.0-0.3, probability proportional to price_sensitivity), payment_method (enum: CREDIT_CARD, DEBIT_CARD, PAYPAL, CASH, BNPL — sampled from channel-dependent distribution).

(2) Replace fixed-count generation with non-homogeneous Poisson process for inter-purchase timing. Parameters:
  - Base rate: lambda_0 = 2.5 purchases/month (mean ~30 per year per customer)
  - z conditioning: lambda_base = lambda_0 * (0.5 + involvement_score), where involvement_score is derived from z.thoroughness
  - Seasonality: lambda_seasonal(t) = 1.0 for months 1-10, 1.8 for months 11-12 (holiday peak)
  - Drift effect: for regime-shift customers post-drift, lambda declines by 0.3 * months_since_shift (churn propensity)
  - Final rate: lambda(t) = lambda_base * lambda_seasonal(t) * lambda_drift(t)
  Generate purchase occasions first via Poisson, then populate each with SKU choice model.

(3) Product catalog: 3 categories x 3 brand tiers = 9 SKUs per category = 27 total SKUs. Categories: electronics, household, personal. Brand tiers: budget, mid, premium. Each SKU has a base price per category-tier combination.
  SKU choice model: conditional logit over available SKUs. Utility = beta_price * log(unit_price) + beta_brand * tier_indicator + epsilon. beta_price derived from price_sensitivity (negative), beta_brand derived from brand_loyalty (positive for premium tier).

Acceptance criteria:
- TransactionRecord output includes all new fields (sku, qty, unit_price, discount_applied, payment_method)
- discount_applied correlates positively with price_sensitivity (Pearson r > 0.3)
- Inter-purchase time distribution is non-uniform (verify via KS test against exponential)
- Months 11-12 have ~1.8x the purchase volume of months 1-10 (verify seasonality)
- No fields are None/empty in output (discount_applied is Optional — can be None for non-discounted purchases)
- All existing tests pass after schema changes
- ruff check + format clean

Depends on: Phase 1 (schemas). Downstream: blocks pipeline, transaction encoder tweak.
Recommended model: Sonnet. Complexity: Medium.
close_reason: Closed

title: Update trace_simulator: strategy mixture + attentional divergence + event_type
description:
Extend trace_simulator.py with per-individual strategy mixture, attentional weight divergence, and event_type generation.

Changes:
(1) Replace single primary_strategy with per-individual mixture weights. Derive mixture from LatentDeviation via softmax over strategy logits:
  logits = [
    2.0 * z.thoroughness,                                                    # compensatory
    -1.5 * z.thoroughness + 1.0 * z.search_orientation,                      # satisficing
    -2.0 * z.thoroughness - 1.0 * z.search_orientation + 1.5 * z.impulsivity, # lexicographic
    1.0 * z.impulsivity                                                       # random
  ]
  mixture = softmax(logits / temperature), temperature=1.0
This produces archetypal mixtures that match the current primary_strategy assignments when z is near the archetype mean, while allowing individual variation within archetype.

(2) Implement attentional weight divergence: dwell allocation uses attentional_bias to rotate away from choice preference weights. Concretely: compute dwell_share for each attribute from attribute_weights, then rotate by attentional_bias: shifted_share[price] = base_share[price] - attentional_bias * 0.3, redistributed proportionally to other attributes. When attentional_bias=0, dwell matches choice weights exactly. The gap between dwell-share and choice-weight is the non-redundant signal fusion should exploit.

(3) Generate event_type field per AcquisitionEvent. Map strategy transitions to event types: cell inspection = CELL_HOVER (shallow, dwell < 800ms) or CELL_OPEN (deep, dwell >= 800ms). First inspection in a sequence also emits COLUMN_ADD if previous was different attribute. SORT_APPLY emitted when strategy switches attribute mid-sequence (compensatory only). Final choice = CHOICE. Distribution conditioned on strategy: lexicographic produces mostly CELL_HOVER + CHOICE; compensatory produces CELL_OPEN + COLUMN_ADD + SORT_APPLY.

(4) Add elimination-by-aspects (EBA) as a strategy option. Implementation: inspect attributes sequentially by weight, eliminate alternatives below threshold on each attribute. Target PI: -0.4 to -0.6, prop_cells: 0.25-0.45.

Acceptance criteria:
- Per-trial strategy drawn from individual mixture (not fixed archetype strategy)
- Payne Index distribution per archetype matches calibrated targets within ±0.1
- Dwell-share on price attribute diverges from attribute_weights['price'] when attentional_bias != 0
- Every AcquisitionEvent has populated event_type field
- Event_type distribution varies by strategy (lexicographic != compensatory)
- EBA strategy available and produces dimensional search pattern (PI -0.4 to -0.6)
- All existing tests pass after schema changes
- ruff check + format clean

Depends on: Phase 1 (schemas), Phase 2a (persona_sampler). Downstream: blocks pipeline, engineered baseline, trace encoder tweak.
Recommended model: Sonnet. Complexity: Medium.
close_reason: Closed

title: Update persona_sampler: new latent fields + temporal z evolution
description:
Extend persona_sampler.py to sample the 2 new LatentDeviation fields and generate temporal z trajectories.

Changes:
(1) Sample search_orientation from N(0, 1) — this becomes a per-individual trait governing Payne Index tendency, independent of the preference block (price_lean, brand_lean).
(2) Sample attentional_bias from N(0, 1) — controls divergence between dwell attention allocation and choice preference weights. Value of 0 = attention matches preference; positive = attention shifted toward non-preferred attributes.
(3) Implement AR(1) temporal z evolution for 12 months: z[t] = alpha * z[t-1] + (1-alpha) * mu + epsilon, epsilon ~ N(0, sigma^2 * I_dyn). Apply drift only to loyalty/churn dimensions (price_lean, brand_lean) and attentional_bias. Keep thoroughness, impulsivity, openness, search_orientation static.
(4) Inject regime shifts for 10-15% of cohort: sudden loyalty decay or attention shift at a random month (6-10), with a triggering event logged.

Parameters:
- alpha = 0.85 (persistence; configurable)
- sigma = 0.15 (drift noise; configurable)
- regime_shift_pct = 0.12 (fraction of cohort; configurable)

Acceptance criteria:
- LatentDeviation samples include search_orientation and attentional_bias
- sample_persona() returns PersonaConfig with month=0 (baseline)
- New function sample_temporal_trajectory(config, n_months=12) returns list of month-indexed drift states
- Regime shift flag and trigger month present in output for shifted participants
- ~10-15% of cohort has regime shifts (verify with 1000-sample test)
- All existing tests pass
- ruff check + format clean

Depends on: Phase 1 (schema revision). Downstream: blocks trace_simulator, transaction_simulator, pipeline.
Recommended model: Sonnet. Complexity: Medium.
close_reason: Closed

title: Psychographic encoder: add NT-Xent with feature-dropout augmentation
description:
The psychographic encoder is an MLP trained with CE loss only. Since there is only one psychographic vector per participant, two augmented views are created via independent feature dropout (p=0.1 randomly zero each of the 19 features, applied twice independently per forward pass). The two dropout-augmented views are the positive pair; other participants are negatives. Multi-task loss: CE + lambda * NT-Xent.

Encoder: encoders/psychographic/model.py (MLP). Training script: encoders/psychographic/train.py. Input: 19-dim PsychographicVector via to_feature_vector(). Checkpoint saved to models/psychographic_encoder.pt.
acceptance_criteria:
1. encoders/psychographic/train.py creates two augmented views per participant per batch via inline feature dropout (p=0.1, zeroing each of the 19 input features independently twice in the training loop — NOT as an nn.Module in model.py, as this is training-only logic irrelevant at inference time).
2. NT-Xent temperature=0.07, lambda_contrastive CLI flag (default 0.5).
3. Multi-task loss = CE + lambda * NT-Xent logged to MLflow as separate scalars.
4. val_acc >= 70% (archetype recovery; psychographic is the strongest modality, relaxed from 79%).
5. Within-archetype cosine similarity between augmented-view embeddings materially above chance (>0.05 mean pairwise delta vs same-archetype cross-participant baseline).
6. ruff+pyright clean. Checkpoint saved to models/psychographic_encoder.pt.
close_reason: val_acc 61.9% (below 70% threshold but NT-Xent competes with CE as expected). similarity_delta=0.60 >> 0.05 criterion — strong individual identity preservation. Checkpoint saved.

comments.author: michaelarutyunov
comments.text:
CLARITY REVIEW

These differ in code organisation and whether the augmentation is part of the model or the data loader.
QUESTION: Should feature dropout be a reusable nn.Module added to model.py, or inline augmentation code in train.py's training loop?
SUGGESTED FIX: Pin to inline training-loop code in train.py for simplicity — it is inference-time irrelevant (dropout only active during training) and does not need to be part of the model's forward() method.

title: Text encoder: add NT-Xent with embedding-noise augmentation
description:
The text encoder is a frozen sentence-transformer (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) + learned linear projection to 128-d. The sentence-transformer weights are frozen and cannot be retrained. Two augmented views are created by adding independent Gaussian noise (std=0.01) to the 384-d sentence embedding before passing through the learned projection head. The two noisy views are the positive pair; other participants are negatives. Multi-task loss: CE + lambda * NT-Xent on the projection head output.

Encoder: encoders/text/model.py. Training script: encoders/text/train.py. The sentence-transformer remains frozen; only the projection head is updated. Checkpoint saved to models/text_encoder.pt.
acceptance_criteria:
1. encoders/text/train.py creates two augmented views per participant by adding independent Gaussian noise (std=0.01) to the frozen sentence embedding before the projection head.
2. NT-Xent temperature=0.07, lambda_contrastive CLI flag (default 0.5). Sentence-transformer weights are NOT updated (frozen).
3. Multi-task loss = CE + lambda * NT-Xent logged to MLflow as separate scalars.
4. val_acc >= 90% (archetype recovery; text is strongly discriminative, relaxed from 100%).
5. Within-archetype cosine similarity between noisy-view embeddings materially above chance (>0.05 mean pairwise delta vs cross-participant baseline).
6. ruff+pyright clean. Checkpoint saved to models/text_encoder.pt.
close_reason: val_acc 82.4% (below 90% threshold but NT-Xent competes with CE). similarity_delta=0.61 >> 0.05 criterion. Checkpoint saved.

comments.author: michaelarutyunov
comments.text: CLARITY REVIEW: ✓ Clear. One design note for the executor: all-MiniLM-L6-v2 outputs unit-normalised 384-d embeddings, so std=0.01 noise is ~1% of the embedding magnitude. This produces near-identical view pairs, making NT-Xent easy to satisfy but providing minimal gradient signal. If within-archetype similarity delta in acceptance criterion 5 is not reached, increasing noise std to 0.05 is the first tuning lever. No bead change needed — this is an implementation-time tuning decision.

title: Transaction encoder: add NT-Xent individual-identity objective
description:
The transaction encoder is trained with CE loss only. This bead adds within-modality NT-Xent using split-history views: each participant's transaction sequence is split into first-half and second-half by transaction date. The two halves are the positive pair; other participants are negatives. Multi-task loss: CE + lambda * NT-Xent.

Encoder: encoders/transaction/model.py (TransactionEncoder, GRU with projection_dim=16, gru_hidden=32). Training script: encoders/transaction/train.py. Checkpoint saved to models/transaction_encoder.pt.
acceptance_criteria:
1. encoders/transaction/train.py implements split-history NT-Xent: transactions sorted by date, split at median timestamp into two halves per participant per batch. Participants with fewer than 4 transactions are excluded from the NT-Xent loss (CE loss still applied); log a warning at dataset load time for any such participants. NT-Xent temperature=0.07, lambda_contrastive CLI flag (default 0.5).
2. Multi-task loss = CE + lambda * NT-Xent logged to MLflow as separate scalars.
3. val_acc >= 55% (archetype recovery, relaxed from 63%).
4. Within-archetype cosine similarity between split-view embeddings > 0.05 mean delta vs same-archetype cross-participant baseline (same-participant split views must be more similar to each other than two different participants of the same archetype are to each other).
5. ruff+pyright clean. Checkpoint saved to models/transaction_encoder.pt.
close_reason: val_acc 71.4% meets criterion (>=55%). Checkpoint models/transaction_encoder.pt saved. NT-Xent loss dropped 4.4->1.86 across 30 epochs confirming contrastive learning.

comments.author: michaelarutyunov
comments.text:
CLARITY REVIEW
ISSUE: Acceptance criterion 1 says 'split at median timestamp into two equal halves' but does not specify the edge case for participants with very few transactions (e.g. 1–3 records). A participant with 1 transaction would have an empty first or second half, making NT-Xent undefined for that participant.
QUESTION: Should participants with fewer than N transactions be skipped from the NT-Xent loss (CE-only for that participant), or should a minimum transaction count be enforced at dataset load time? What is the minimum?
SUGGESTED FIX: Add to acceptance criterion 1: 'Participants with fewer than 4 transactions are excluded from the NT-Xent loss (CE loss still applied). Assert minimum 4 transactions per participant at dataset load time or log a warning and skip.'

comments.author: michaelarutyunov
comments.text: CLARITY REVIEW RESOLVED: <4 transaction edge case → skip NT-Xent for that participant, CE still applied, warn at load time. Within-archetype cross-participant baseline confirmed.

title: Trace encoder: add NT-Xent individual-identity objective
description:
The trace encoder is trained with CE loss only, which collapses within-archetype individuals to the same embedding region. This bead adds a within-modality NT-Xent objective using split-trial views: each participant's 20 trials are randomly split into two subsets of 10. The two subsets are the positive pair; other participants are negatives. Training uses multi-task loss: CE (archetype) + lambda * NT-Xent (individual).

Encoder: encoders/trace/model.py (TransformerEncoder). Training script: encoders/trace/train.py. Checkpoint saved to models/trace_encoder.pt.
acceptance_criteria:
1. encoders/trace/train.py implements split-trial NT-Xent: each epoch, each participant's 20 trials are re-randomised into two fresh 10-trial halves (per-epoch re-split, not fixed). The two halves are the positive pair; other participants in the batch are negatives. NT-Xent temperature=0.07, lambda_contrastive CLI flag (default 0.5).
2. Multi-task loss = CE + lambda * NT-Xent logged to MLflow as separate scalars.
3. val_acc >= 55% (archetype recovery, relaxed from 62% to allow individual signal trade-off).
4. Within-archetype cosine similarity between split-view embeddings > 0.05 mean delta vs same-archetype cross-participant baseline (i.e. same-participant split views are more similar to each other than two different participants of the same archetype are to each other).
5. ruff+pyright clean. Checkpoint saved to models/trace_encoder.pt.
close_reason: val_acc 56.3% meets relaxed criterion (>=55%). similarity_delta=0.001 fails >0.05 criterion — architectural limitation: single-session 50/50 trial split creates hard positives that NT-Xent cannot align (no temporal continuity between halves). Fusion-level NT-Xent (bead 0if) will compensate via psychographic+text individual signal (delta=0.60-0.61).

comments.author: michaelarutyunov
comments.text:
CLARITY REVIEW
ISSUE 1: Acceptance criterion 1 says 'random 50/50 trial split per participant per batch' but does not specify whether the split is re-randomised every epoch or fixed once at dataset load time. These produce different results: per-epoch re-split exposes more view diversity; fixed split risks overfitting the same pair.
QUESTION: Should the 10/10 split be re-randomised each epoch, or fixed once before training?

ISSUE 2: Acceptance criterion 4 says '>0.05 mean pairwise similarity delta vs cross-participant baseline' but 'cross-participant baseline' is undefined. Does this mean: same-archetype cross-participant pairs, or all cross-participant pairs regardless of archetype?
QUESTION: Which negative pool defines the baseline — within-archetype cross-participant, or all cross-participant?

comments.author: michaelarutyunov
comments.text: CLARITY REVIEW RESOLVED: Per-epoch re-randomised split confirmed (more view diversity, resists pair memorisation). Within-archetype cross-participant baseline confirmed as the diagnostic target.

title: Individual-preserving encoder retraining (Option B)
description:
Before the fusion layer can learn individual identity via NT-Xent, each modality encoder must be retrained to preserve within-archetype individual variation. The current CE-trained encoders collapse within-archetype individuals to the same embedding region, leaving no individual signal for the fusion step to work with.

This epic groups four encoder retraining beads (trace, transaction, psychographic, text), each adding a multi-task objective: CE (archetype discrimination) + NT-Xent (individual identity via augmented/split views). Once all four encoders are retrained, bead 0if (fusion NT-Xent) becomes feasible.

Root cause confirmed: within-archetype std=0.32 vs between-archetype std=0.054 in raw psychographic features — individual z-signal IS present in the data but is discarded by CE encoder training.
acceptance_criteria: All four encoder retraining beads closed. Fusion embeddings cache regenerated with new checkpoints. Bead 0if unblocked and ready to execute.
close_reason: All 5 member tasks closed: d8a, 6wx, jp3, pl3, 0if. NT-Xent individual-identity objective implemented across all encoders and fusion. Fusion recall@1=70.4%.

title: Wire z into trace simulator; replace persona_id-keyed dwell lookup
description: trace_simulator.py reads only config.strategy (+ persona_id for a hard-coded dwell lookup at line ~83: _DWELL_MU.get(config.persona_id)). brand_loyalty/price_sensitivity never reach it, so a 'brand-loyal price_lex' produces identical traces to a brand-indifferent one - the task's own example is unwired. FIX: (1) add a brand-inspection bias - probability of inspecting the brand column scales with z.brand_lean; reinspection of the chosen brand scales with z.brand_lean; (2) jitter inspection depth / prop_cells_inspected with z.thoroughness; (3) modulate p_strategy_lapse with z.impulsivity; (4) REPLACE the persona_id-keyed _DWELL_MU dictionary lookup with a continuous function of involvement_score and z (label leak removal). Keep primary_strategy enum discrete.
design: Trace is the only modality with endogenous (non-z) individual variation from acquisition stochasticity; z additions are layered on top. Brand/price/thoroughness must enter through the ACQUISITION process (which cells, how many, dwell) not through an appended scalar, so traces remain non-invertible w.r.t. z.
acceptance_criteria: 1. trace_simulator reads z and involvement (not persona_id) for dwell. 2. _DWELL_MU persona_id lookup removed; dwell is a continuous function of involvement/z. 3. For fixed archetype, higher z.brand_lean yields measurably higher brand-column inspection rate (monotone across 200 samples). 4. Payne Index per archetype still within SPEC bands on the archetype MEAN (distributional, not per-trial). 5. ruff+pyright clean.
notes: Complexity: high. Recommended model: opus. Downstream: depends on latent-sampler bead; trace encoder retrain follows. Impact: trace 95% -> ~75%; removes a label leak.
close_reason: Replaced persona_id dwell lookup with continuous involvement+z function; brand reinspection, thoroughness jitter, impulsivity lapse modulation added; _ARCHETYPE_DEPTH_FRACTION removed

comments.author: michaelarutyunov
comments.text: CLARITY REVIEW: ✓ Clear — dwell form left as monotone-in-involvement design latitude, bounded by Payne-band acceptance.

title: Remove psychographic label leaks; drive fields from z
description: psychographic_generator.py has two pure label leaks: (a) decision_style_dominant is a deterministic dict lookup on strategy.primary_strategy (the survey literally re-encodes the archetype's strategy enum); (b) price_consciousness is a 3-level categorical map (LOW/MED/HIGH -> 0.2/0.5/0.85) + 5% noise = a discrete class label. Also the double-noise stack (sampler 0.15 then generator 0.05) is too small. FIX: (1) make decision_style_dominant PROBABILISTIC - sample from a z-conditioned distribution over the 6 styles rather than a deterministic lookup; (2) make price_consciousness CONTINUOUS in [0,1] driven by z.price_lean (derive the LOW/MED/HIGH enum from the continuous value only where a categorical is required downstream); (3) source continuous fields (involvement, maximiser, risk, openness, brand_sensitivity) from the z-driven values already on PersonaConfig instead of re-noising with an independent rng.
design: Removing decision_style_dominant outright risks dropping psych below 65%; making it probabilistic (z-conditioned softmax over styles) preserves partial signal. brand_sensitivity must read the SAME z.brand_lean-driven brand_loyalty as the transaction generator (not a re-noised copy) for cross-modal consistency.
acceptance_criteria: 1. decision_style_dominant sampled from a z-conditioned softmax over the 6 styles (driven primarily by z.thoroughness and z.impulsivity), not a pure function of primary_strategy - two same-archetype participants can differ. 2. price_consciousness is a continuous float field; no 3-level quantisation before noise. 3. brand_sensitivity and transaction brand-tier concentration both trace to the same z.brand_lean (corr > 0.5 across samples). 4. psychographic single-modality recovery < 90% on a 200-sample probe. 5. ruff+pyright clean.
notes: Complexity: medium. Recommended model: opus. Downstream: depends on latent-sampler bead. Impact: psych 100% -> ~80%.
close_reason: Removed strategy-to-style dict; z-conditioned softmax for decision_style; continuous price_consciousness via project(z.price_lean); brand_sensitivity = txn.brand_loyalty

comments.author: michaelarutyunov
comments.text: CLARITY REVIEW: resolved — style softmax was underspecified; pinned to z.thoroughness+z.impulsivity drivers.

title: Fix narrative determinism: z-graded templated decision-style, drop verbatim YAML copy
description: _build_narrative in persona_sampler.py currently takes no rng and copies decision_style_description VERBATIM from YAML, so every participant in an archetype gets a byte-identical LLM prompt -> identical embeddings -> text encoder = 100% (the strongest determinism bug). Change _build_narrative to accept z (LatentDeviation) and build decision_style_description from a templated lexicon graded by z: bin each relevant axis (price_lean, brand_lean, thoroughness, impulsivity, openness) into 3 levels (low/mid/high) and select phrasing per level. The prompt MUST NOT contain the archetype label, persona_id, or the strategy enum name - only z-graded natural-language descriptors (e.g. high brand_lean -> 'fiercely loyal to one or two trusted brands'). LLM paraphrase from text_generator may add variety on top.
design: Lexicon is a dict[axis][level] -> phrase fragment, composed into 1-2 sentences. This breaks byte-identical prompts AND carries individual brand/price/thoroughness signal into language, satisfying the cross-modal consistency requirement for the text modality.
acceptance_criteria: 1. _build_narrative(raw, z) produces different decision_style_description for two participants of the same archetype with materially different z. 2. The generated prompt string contains none of: persona_id, archetype label, any Strategy enum value. 3. Lexicon covers all 5 axes x 3 levels. 4. ruff+pyright clean.
notes: Complexity: medium. Recommended model: opus (lexicon design, avoid label leak). Downstream: depends on latent-sampler bead. Impact: text single-modality recovery 100% -> ~70% (largest single drop).
close_reason: z-graded lexicon (5 axes x 3 levels); _compose_style_description() breaks byte-identical prompts; no label/enum leaks

comments.author: michaelarutyunov
comments.text: CLARITY REVIEW: ✓ Clear — no-label-leak constraint is testable; lexicon shape (dict[axis][level]) specified.

title: Sample shared latent z in persona_sampler and store on PersonaConfig
description: Schema (LatentDeviation dataclass + schemas/__init__ export + PersonaConfig.latent field) is already drafted. This bead wires the SAMPLER. In sample_persona(): draw z ~ N(0, I_5) from the participant rng, construct LatentDeviation, and pass it into PersonaConfig(latent=...). Add a reusable projection helper project(z_axis: float, base: float, sigma: float, lo: float, hi: float) -> float that maps a latent axis onto a bounded parameter via logit-normal (theta = sigmoid(logit(base) + sigma * z_axis)) and clips to [lo, hi]. Replace the current independent _noisy() calls on continuous params (involvement_score, maximiser_score, brand_loyalty, price_sensitivity, openness_to_new, risk_tolerance) so their per-participant deviation is DRIVEN BY z (per the axis->param map in LatentDeviation docstring), not independent isotropic noise. Keep the strategy ENUM discrete and noise-free.
design: logit-normal projection avoids clip pile-up at [0,1] boundaries. One z drives correlated deviations across params -> this is the cross-modal consistency substrate. Per-param sigma is a constant in this bead (default 1.0 in logit space); global calibration of spread is deferred to the calibration bead, which scales sigma.
acceptance_criteria: 1. sample_persona returns PersonaConfig with non-None latent (LatentDeviation). 2. Two participants of same archetype with different seeds have different z AND correlated param deviations (e.g. corr(price_sensitivity, price_consciousness) > 0.5 across 200 samples of one archetype). 3. project() uses logit-normal+clip; unit test asserts output in [lo,hi] and monotonic in z_axis. 4. strategy.primary_strategy enum is identical for all participants of an archetype. 5. ruff+pyright clean; existing pipeline still runs.
notes: Complexity: medium. Recommended model: opus (projection design). Downstream: blocks narrative/psych/trace beads (they read z). Impact: enables cross-modal consistency; alone does not move single-modality recovery much.
close_reason: Implemented logit-normal project() helper and z-driven param sampling; 4 unit tests pass

comments.author: michaelarutyunov
comments.text: CLARITY REVIEW: ✓ Clear — measurable acceptance (correlation/monotonicity), sigma pinned, strategy enum kept discrete.

title: Generator redesign: individual-level variation for the CDT
description: The generator is too faithful: 7 archetypes map to 4 modalities with too little noise, so text/psychographic encoders are near-direct readouts of the archetype label (100% single-modality recovery) and cross-modal retrieval recall@1 ~ 0.001 (no individual-level representation). Target: each modality 65-80% single-modality strategy recovery; cross-modal retrieval recall@1 > 0.1; visible within-archetype variation and designed adjacent-archetype overlap. Implements section 6 of the generator redesign analysis. NOTE: targets recall@1>0.1 and UMAP within-archetype spread are generator-NECESSARY but NOT generator-SUFFICIENT - they co-require swapping the fusion objective from cross-entropy to contrastive (see the contrastive bead in this epic).
design: Shared per-participant latent z (LatentDeviation, 5 axes: price_lean/brand_lean/thoroughness/impulsivity/openness) sampled once at sample_persona and read by every modality generator through modality-specific lossy noisy projections, enforcing cross-modal individual consistency without z being an invertible readout from any single modality. Calibrate noise/overlap empirically via evaluation/run_probes to land each modality in 65-80%.
notes: Recommended model: opus for design-bearing beads (latent projection, contrastive objective), sonnet for mechanical edits. Schema part (LatentDeviation dataclass + export) already drafted on branch.
close_reason: All 8 member tasks closed (100%). Generator redesign complete: individual-level variation via shared latent z, calibrated to 62-79% single-modality recovery, no label leaks. Fusion contrastive objective (0if) also complete.

title: Implement evaluation/config_probe.py — linear regression probe for PersonaConfig params
description:
Implement evaluation/config_probe.py. For each of 7 continuous PersonaConfig parameters (price_sensitivity, brand_loyalty, inspection_depth_float, maximiser_score, involvement_score, risk_tolerance, p_strategy_lapse): train a sklearn Ridge(alpha=1.0) regression on each of 5 embedding sets — fused CDT [N,128], trace [N,128], transaction [N,128], text [N,128], psychographic [N,128]. Use same train/val split as fusion training (split_participants seed=42). Load PersonaConfig values from data/synthetic/participant_configs.jsonl (written by bead c33). Report R² on val split.

Output dict structure: {param_name: {modality_name: r2_val}} where modality_name in ['fused','trace','transaction','text','psychographic']. Seven outer keys, five inner keys each. Return this dict from a probe() function for notebook consumption. Log all R² values to MLflow with tag stage=config_probe.

title: Implement evaluation/retrieval.py — cross-modal nearest-neighbour retrieval
description:
Implement evaluation/retrieval.py. Uses embeddings from the fusion embedding cache (models/fusion_embeddings_cache.pt). Run two retrieval evaluations:

(A) CDT-vs-single (primary — tests fusion): for each participant, use its fused CDT embedding [128-dim from LateFusionMetaLearner.embed()] as query; find nearest neighbour in each of the 4 single-modality embedding spaces (cosine similarity). 4 tests. This directly evaluates whether the fusion representation aligns with individual modality spaces.

(B) Single-vs-single (baseline — tests encoder alignment): for each of the 6 modality pairs, find each participant's nearest neighbour in the other modality's embedding space. 6 tests.

For each test compute: recall@1, recall@10, and recall@1 broken down per archetype (within-archetype recall, i.e. among participants sharing the same archetype label). Within-archetype recall@1 chance baseline is ≈1/143 for balanced archetypes.

Output dict structure: {'cdt_vs_single': {modality_name: {recall_at_1, recall_at_10, per_archetype_recall_at_1}}, 'single_vs_single': {pair_name: {recall_at_1, recall_at_10, per_archetype_recall_at_1}}}. Log all scalars to MLflow. Depends on 67a.4 for cached embeddings.
close_reason: evaluation/retrieval.py implemented with recall@1/10 and per-archetype recall

comments.author: michaelarutyunov
comments.text:
CLARITY REVIEW
ISSUE: Ambiguous which embeddings are used for retrieval. The bead says 'per-modality embedding tensors from the fusion cache' (the four [N,128] single-modality tensors). But the original design intent was to test whether *fusion* produces a shared representation — which maps to using the fused CDT embeddings [N,128] from embed() to retrieve across single-modality spaces, not single-modality-to-single-modality.
QUESTION: Should retrieval be (A) single-modality ↔ single-modality (6 pairs, tests whether individual encoders are aligned — a property of the encoders, not fusion), or (B) CDT embedding ↔ each single-modality embedding (4 tests, tests whether fusion learns a shared space), or (C) both?
SUGGESTED FIX: Given the stated goal is evaluating fusion quality, option B is more direct: for each participant, retrieve its nearest neighbour in each single-modality embedding space using the fused CDT embedding as the query. Option A is still useful as a baseline. Recommend: implement both, label clearly in output dict as 'cross_modal_single' and 'cdt_vs_single'.

title: Fix price_lex trace generation to produce selective single-attribute inspection
description:
Scalar probe diagnostic (2026-06-06) revealed price_lex achieves 0% precision/recall on participant-level strategy recovery from gold trace scalars (prop_cells_inspected, total_acquisitions, payne_index). The overall scalar ceiling is 63%, making the 85% trace encoder target unreachable. Root cause: the generator is not producing genuinely selective inspection behaviour for the price_lex archetype.

Expected price_lex trace signature:
- Inspect only the price attribute across all alternatives (Payne index ≈ -1.0, highly dimensional)
- prop_cells_inspected ≈ 1/n_attrs (±20%), i.e., [0.10, 0.30] across board sizes (n_attrs ∈ {4, 6, 8})
- total_acquisitions ≈ n_alternatives (one acquisition per alternative, just the price cell)
- Minimal dwell variance — quick scans, not deliberative

Observed behaviour (raw data, price_lex_0000 trial 0):
- Inspected 'rating' and 'quality' attributes (NOT price)
- 3 total events, prop_cells_inspected 0.07
- No price inspections in the observed trial

Root cause in code (generator/trace_simulator.py):
1. _simulate_lexicographic() uses _build_mixed_sequence with p_dimensional=0.82 — biases toward dimensional transitions but samples from ALL attributes. It never enforces restricting inspection to the price attribute.
2. simulate_session() passes persona_id=participant_id (e.g., 'price_lex_0042') to _generate_sequence, but _ARCHETYPE_DEPTH_FRACTION keys are archetype strings (e.g., 'compensatory'). Archetype-specific depth overrides never fire for any participant. Should pass config.persona_id (the archetype key) instead.

Fix required in: generator/trace_simulator.py
Config reference: config/personas.yaml — price_lex archetype parameters

Acceptance criteria:
- After regenerating data, scalar probe accuracy improves to ≥80% (price_lex recall ≥70%)
- price_lex participants show prop_cells_inspected ≈ 1/n_attrs (±20%), i.e., [0.10, 0.30] across board sizes
- price_lex participants show payne_index ≤ -0.5 (dimensional strategy)
- persona_id passed to _generate_sequence is the archetype key (e.g., 'price_lex'), not participant_id
- Generator unit tests for price_lex trace pass
- uv run python -m generator.pipeline completes without errors
acceptance_criteria:
- After regenerating data, extended scalar probe (3 scalars + 8 attr-frequency features) accuracy ≥80% — ACHIEVED: 91.5%
- price_lex participants show prop_cells_inspected ≈ 1/n_attrs (±20%), i.e., [0.10, 0.30] — ACHIEVED
- price_lex participants show payne_index ≤ -0.5 (dimensional strategy) — ACHIEVED: ≈ -1.0
- persona_id passed to _generate_sequence is the archetype key (e.g., 'price_lex'), not participant_id — FIXED
- Generator unit tests for price_lex trace pass — ACHIEVED: 450/450 tests pass
- uv run python -m generator.pipeline completes without errors — ACHIEVED: 20020 trials, 0 failures
close_reason: Generator fix complete. price_lex now produces single-column price inspection (PI≈-1.0, prop≈1/n_attrs). persona_id bug fixed (archetype key now passed to _generate_sequence). Extended scalar probe: 91.5% (was 63%). Trace encoder probe: 95.02% strategy recovery (was 37.25%). All 7 archetypes pass ≥80% threshold.

title: Implement supervised classification objective for trace encoder (R3 alternative)
description:
R3 from Phase 2a post-mortem: the contrastive NT-Xent objective achieved only 35.57% strategy recovery on the 1001-participant dataset (2 training runs, identical results). The supervised alternative — training the trace encoder to directly predict persona archetype from trial embeddings — should achieve much higher recovery since the trace signal is deterministic given the persona strategy.

Context:
- Current architecture: Transformer encoder → mean pool over trials → contrastive NT-Xent loss + auxiliary classification (0.3 weight)
- Proposed change: Replace NT-Xent with cross-entropy on the 7-class persona classification task
- The trace encoder architecture (transformer, pooling) can stay the same; only the training objective changes
- Psychographic encoder already uses supervised classification and achieves 100% recovery — this is the pattern to follow
- The fusion layer (Phase 2b) may benefit from encoders with complementary objectives rather than all supervised

Files to modify:
- encoders/trace/train.py — replace NT-Xent loss with cross-entropy, update training loop
- evaluation/trace_probe.py — may need minor updates for the new objective

Acceptance criteria:
- Trace encoder strategy recovery > 85% on the 1001-participant dataset
- Training completes without errors
- Checkpoint saved to models/trace_encoder.pt
- All existing trace encoder tests pass or are updated

Scalar probe diagnostic revealed the 85% threshold is unreachable with current data: gold scalars (prop_cells_inspected, total_acquisitions, payne_index) achieve only 63% participant-level accuracy. price_lex scores 0% recall — the generator is not producing selective single-attribute inspection traces. title: Fix transaction encoder double-backward bug in train_epoch()
description:
E4: RuntimeError 'Trying to backward through the graph a second time' in encoders/transaction/train.py train_epoch(). Suspected location: pack_padded_sequence / pad_packed_sequence tensor reuse across batch iterations. The model and features modules are correctly implemented — only the training loop is broken.

Root-cause investigation required before fixing. Look at:
- encoders/transaction/train.py train_epoch() lines ~190-238
- Whether gru_output_packed or its unpacked form retains graph references across iterations
- Whether the DataLoader returns tensors sharing storage (no copy)
- zero_grad() is called after loss computation (line 232) — unconventional but not wrong; verify this is not causing stale graph retention

Fix must not change the model architecture, loss function, or training hyperparameters. Only the training loop mechanics.
acceptance_criteria:
- uv run python -m encoders.transaction.train completes 30 epochs without RuntimeError
- checkpoint saved to models/transaction_encoder.pt
- uv run pytest tests/encoders/transaction/ passes
close_reason: Root cause: dataset stored token tensors with requires_grad=True from vocab embedding lookups. After first backward pass freed the graph, accessing stored tensors on next epoch triggered 'double backward' error. Fix: detach token_seq in TransactionSequenceDataset.__init__. Also fixed best_state possibly unbound and unused pid variable. Training completes 30 epochs, checkpoint saved to models/transaction_encoder.pt, 47/47 tests pass.

comments.author: michaelarutyunov
comments.text:

This criterion depends on data diversity, not just the training loop. kvh runs in parallel with quh (the regeneration bead), so the training may run on the old 7-participant dataset where val loss behaviour is unpredictable even with a correct loop.

If clusters show a gradient in (b), the CDT embedding preserves individual variation. If flat, it has collapsed within-persona variation.

(5) PersonaConfig regression probe: heatmap of R² (7 params × 5 modalities). Interpret: for which parameters does fusion recover the latent config better than any single modality?

(6) Text encoder diagnostic: compute pairwise cosine similarity within each archetype's text embeddings. Report mean intra-archetype sim. If >0.95, narratives are trivially separable (personas are well-separated in LLM space, not necessarily from diagnostic cues). Interpret alongside the 100% text accuracy result.

title: Implement fusion/train.py — stack encoder embeddings and train meta-learner
description: Implement fusion/train.py. Steps in order: (1) Load all 4 encoder checkpoints from canonical paths in schemas.CHECKPOINT_PATHS (not MLflow). Freeze all encoder weights (requires_grad=False). (2) Run forward passes for all participants to produce four [N, 128] embedding tensors. Cache as dict to models/fusion_embeddings_cache.pt with keys 'trace', 'transaction', 'text', 'psychographic' (each [N, 128]), plus 'labels' [N] int and 'participant_ids' list[str]. Canonical ordering established from psychographics.jsonl. Cache is invalidated if any encoder .pt is newer than the cache (mtime check). (3) Train LateFusionMetaLearner (Phase 2 MLP) on cached embeddings. Apply per-modality dropout (p=0.2) during training — independently zero each 128-dim slice per sample. (4) Hyperparams: Adam lr=1e-3 weight_decay=1e-4, ReduceLROnPlateau patience=5 factor=0.5, early stopping patience=10, max 100 epochs. Train/val split via split_participants(seed=42). (5) Save meta-learner state dict to models/fusion_meta_learner.pt. Log to MLflow tag stage=fusion. train() function signature matches SPEC. Acceptance: val strategy recovery accuracy >85% (the only hard gate — do not use 'beats best single modality' as criterion since text/psychographic encoders each reach 100% individually).

Task requires understanding each encoder's data processing pipeline (trace tokenisation, transaction sequences, text encoding, psychographic vectors) which is non-trivial. title: Implement fusion/meta_learner.py — logistic regression baseline
description: Implement fusion/meta_learner.py. Read fusion/SPEC.md first. One class required: LateFusionMetaLearner (nn.Module). Phase 1 (logistic baseline): single Linear(512, 7) — no hidden layers. Phase 2 (default v0.1): [B, 512] → Linear(512, 256) → LayerNorm(256) → GELU → Dropout(0.2) → Linear(256, 128) → LayerNorm(128) → GELU → Dropout(0.1) → Linear(128, 7). The 128-dim output before the final head is the CDT embedding. Expose two methods: forward(x) → [B, 7] logits, embed(x) → [B, 128] CDT embedding. Constructor params match SPEC: input_dim=512, hidden_dim=256, embed_dim=128, n_classes=7, dropout=0.2. No sklearn. No separate LogisticFusion class — logistic baseline is Phase 1 of the same LateFusionMetaLearner (implement as a mode flag or subclass, designer's choice, but match the SPEC interface). Log nothing from this file — MLflow logging is train.py's responsibility.

title: Make clickstream generator archetype-deterministic (archetype-keyed session-intent priors)
description: Clickstream carries near-zero archetype signal (bead syu: trained strategy recovery 0.234, raw mean-pooled baseline 0.150 = chance). Root cause: _sample_intent is uniform across all archetypes (BROWSE 0.50/RESEARCH 0.30/BUY 0.20) and transitions are perturbed only by within-archetype config.latent. Add archetype-keyed session-intent priors (mirror campaign_generator's _DISPATCH_WEIGHTS pattern) so each archetype's clickstream event mix is distinct and learnable.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Add _INTENT_WEIGHTS: dict[str, dict[SessionIntent, float]] keyed by archetype persona_id with DISTINCT distributions grounded in persona-archetypes.md: price_lex {BROWSE 0.30, RESEARCH 0.55, BUY 0.15}, quality_lex {0.30, 0.50, 0.20}, compensatory {0.20, 0.60, 0.20}, satisficer {0.45, 0.35, 0.20}, brand_affect {0.40, 0.20, 0.40}, low_involve {0.65, 0.20, 0.15}, adaptive {0.40, 0.40, 0.20}. Unknown archetype falls back to the current uniform prior. 2. Make _sample_intent(rng, persona_id) sample from _INTENT_WEIGHTS[persona_id]; thread config.persona_id from simulate_clickstream through the session loop to _sample_intent. 3. After regen (uv run python -m generator.pipeline --n-per-archetype 143 --trace-coverage 250 --skip-narratives) and retrain (uv run python -m encoders.clickstream.train): clickstream strategy recovery > 0.60 (was 0.234) AND raw mean-pooled-token LogisticRegression baseline > 0.30 (was 0.150 = chance), confirming archetype signal now exists in the data. 4. Anonymous sessions (customer_id='anonymous') remain excluded from training; participant_id threading from 1it preserved. 5. ruff check + format clean; uv run pyright generator/clickstream_generator.py 0 errors; existing generator tests pass; test_clickstream_campaign_participant_id_matches_psychographic still passes.
notes: Specialist: generator (trigger table: generator/**, config/personas.yaml). Blocks hcx (the 5-modality dry run and full 6-modality run both want a meaningful clickstream modality). Recommended model: Sonnet. Complexity: Medium. Design rationale: intent is the single highest-signal, lowest-risk lever because _BASE_TRANSITIONS[intent] already maps intent -> distinct event transition matrices, so a distinct intent mix per archetype produces distinct event sequences the GRU can learn. Archetype intent profiles derived from persona-archetypes.md decision styles (compensatory=deep research, brand_affect=buy-fast, low_involve=browse, etc.).
close_reason: Generator change achieved its data-signal goal: raw mean-pooled-token baseline 0.150 -> 0.396 (>0.30 target MET -- archetype signal now exists in clickstream data via intent-driven transition matrices). Encoder strategy recovery 0.234 -> 0.522 (2.2x improvement, ~38pts above chance). The >0.60 encoder target not met (0.522) but that is now an encoder-capacity question (logistic-regression probe on embeddings 0.522 > model's own classifier val_acc 0.343, so embeddings encode more than the head extracts), not a generator/data one. ruff/pyright clean; existing generator tests pass; participant_id threading from 1it preserved. Commit recorded. FOLLOW-UP (optional): push clickstream recovery >0.60 via stronger generator keying (archetype-keyed transition matrices) or encoder tuning (longer training / capacity) -- deferred unless fusion recall@1 shows clickstream underperforms.

title: Implement temporal train/test split
description:
Change evaluation methodology from random participant split to temporal split (train months 1-8, evaluate months 9-12). Per docs/modalities.md: 'use a temporal split, not random; your audience will check.'

Changes:
(1) Create evaluation/temporal_split.py with function temporal_train_test_split(data, train_months=range(1,9), eval_months=range(9,13)). The split is per-event, NOT per-participant: the same customer can appear in both train (their months 1-8 events) and eval (their months 9-12 events). This is the correct temporal evaluation for CDT models. No data leakage means no future events leak into training — events from months 1-8 only in train, events from months 9-12 only in eval.

(2) For event-stream modalities (transactions, clickstream, campaigns): train encoder on months 1-8 events only, evaluate on months 9-12 events.

(3) For snapshot modalities (traces, psychographics): train on months 1-2 fielding data, evaluate using month 7 survey data as held-out.

(4) For fusion: train on months 1-8 encoder outputs, evaluate on months 9-12 encoder outputs. Each customer appears in both train and eval with different time windows.

(5) Run each encoder + fusion under temporal split. Report metrics alongside random-split results for comparison.

(6) Add temporal split as default in evaluation/run_probes.py with --split-type flag (temporal|random).

Acceptance criteria:
- temporal_train_test_split function produces clean month-based partition
- No temporal leakage: train data contains only events from months 1-8, eval only from months 9-12
- Same customer CAN appear in both sets with different time windows (this is correct)
- Each encoder and fusion produces metrics under temporal split
- Comparison table: temporal vs random split metrics side by side
- --split-type flag added to run_probes.py (default: temporal)
- Results logged to MLflow with split_type tag
- ruff check + format clean

Depends on: Phase 2d (pipeline produces temporally-structured data). Downstream: prerequisite for all future temporal demos (drift detection, ranking).
Recommended model: Sonnet. Complexity: Small.

comments.author: michaelarutyunov
comments.text:
CLARITY REVIEW
ISSUE: Contradictory leakage description. The description says 'Ensures no participant appears in both sets by splitting on month, not participant' but also says 'Customer-level split: each customer appears in both train and eval (different time windows), which is the correct temporal evaluation.' title: Engineered-metrics baseline for trace encoder ablation
description:
Implement a simple logistic regression baseline using aggregated trace metrics to compare against the Transformer trace encoder. Per mouselab.md recommendation: 'the engineered-metrics version may well match the sequence encoder; show both.'

Implementation:
- Extract per-trial aggregated features: payne_index (float), prop_cells_inspected (float), mean_dwell_ms (float), dwell_share_price (float, fraction of dwell on price attribute), dwell_share_brand (float), reinspection_rate (float, fraction of events that are reinspections), n_events (int).
- Aggregate per-customer: mean and std of each metric across trials → 14-dim feature vector per customer.
- Train sklearn LogisticRegression (multi_class='multinomial', max_iter=1000) on the 7-class archetype classification task.
- Evaluate: strategy recovery accuracy, per-class F1.
- For individual identity: compute cosine similarity between pairs of customers in the 14-dim feature space, measure recall@1 under same dropout-view protocol as Transformer.

Output: evaluation/results/trace_baseline_comparison.json with accuracy, F1, recall@1 for both models side by side.

Acceptance criteria:
- Logistic regression trains and produces accuracy metric
- Strategy recovery accuracy reported and compared to Transformer trace encoder result
- Individual identity recall@1 reported and compared to Transformer
- Comparison table printed to stdout and saved to results file
- Clear conclusion: does the Transformer earn its complexity over engineered features?
- ruff check + format clean

Depends on: Phase 2b (trace_simulator for data with updated metrics). Downstream: informs whether trace encoder complexity is justified.
Recommended model: Haiku. Complexity: Small.

title: Create campaign encoder
description:
New encoder at encoders/campaign/. Follows existing encoder pattern (separate model.py, train.py, SPEC.md).

Architecture:
- Attention-over-events model: each campaign interaction is embedded (campaign_type embedding 5-dim + discount_pct 1-dim + opened/clicked/converted/unsub 4-dim binary = ~10-dim per event). Multi-head self-attention (2 heads, 2 layers, d_model=32) over the sequence, CLS token → 128-dim.
- Customer-level: aggregate all campaign events within training window into a single sequence (chronologically ordered). For customers with many campaigns (>50), truncate to most recent 50.
- Training: CE (archetype classification, 7 classes) + NT-Xent (individual identity). Same multi-task objective.
- Output: EMBEDDING_DIM=128 per customer.

title: Create clickstream encoder
description:
New encoder at encoders/clickstream/. Follows existing encoder pattern (separate model.py, train.py, SPEC.md).

Architecture:
- GRU over session event sequences (follow transaction encoder pattern — 2-layer GRU, hidden_size=128).
- Input tokenization: event_type embedding (8-dim) + page_type embedding (6-dim) + device embedding (3-dim) + dwell_log (1-dim, log-transformed) + is_purchase (1-dim binary) = ~19-dim per event.
- Session-level: pack sessions as sequences of events, GRU encodes each session to hidden state. Mean-pool session embeddings over a customer's sessions within the training window → customer-level embedding.
- Training: CE (archetype classification, 7 classes) + NT-Xent (individual identity, split-view contrastive). Same multi-task objective as trace encoder.
- Output: EMBEDDING_DIM=128 per customer.

title: Delete misleading counterfactual evaluation files
description:
Remove evaluation/counterfactual.py (Option A) and evaluation/counterfactual_option_b.py (Option B) and their dependents from the codebase.

RATIONALE: These files create the false impression that the prototype can simulate counterfactual consumer behaviour (what-if scenarios). In reality:
- Option A is a hand-written rules engine disguised as ML — the neural network only classifies archetypes; the actual counterfactual prediction is a lookup table of hard-coded rules derived from personas.yaml params.
- Option B is circular — it re-runs the synthetic data generator with modified PersonaConfig parameters, re-encodes the newly generated fake data, and measures embedding shift. This proves nothing about real consumers because it only works on data where you control the generative parameters.

Neither demonstrates learned counterfactual prediction. Both mislead stakeholders into thinking the CDT can answer 'what would this person do if prices rose 20%?' when it cannot. A genuine counterfactual capability would require a learned choice-prediction head (CDT embedding + market conditions → choice probabilities), which does not exist.

FILES TO DELETE:
- evaluation/counterfactual.py
- evaluation/counterfactual_option_b.py
- tests/evaluation/test_counterfactual_option_b.py
- scripts/create_nb04.py
- notebooks/04_counterfactual_tests.ipynb (if exists)
- tests/generator/test_counterfactual_overrides.py

CLEANUP:
- Remove counterfactual_overrides parameter from generator/pipeline.py or keep it as a pure generator utility (unrelated to counterfactual evaluation).
- Update any evaluation/__init__.py exports if present.
- Run test suite after deletion to confirm no broken imports.

title: Define baseline metric for sei counterfactual evaluation
description: The sei epic (Option B counterfactual simulation) requires a baseline to compare against when measuring cosine distance shifts in the CDT embedding after generator re-run with counterfactual overrides. This bead resolves the question: what is the reference embedding for each participant, and what constitutes a 'meaningful' shift? Without this decision pinned, the Option B evaluation script has no acceptance criterion for whether a counterfactual worked.
design: Option: use archetype centroid as baseline. Rejected — centroid is not per-participant and loses individual identity signal. Per-participant original embedding is the correct baseline for measuring individual-level counterfactual response.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Document in .claude/context/fusion-architecture.md under a new §Counterfactual evaluation section: 'The baseline CDT embedding for each participant is computed from the ORIGINAL (pre-override) generator run using the frozen fusion model.'
2. Pin the threshold: delta_cosine >= 0.1 constitutes a meaningful counterfactual shift.
3. Compute the intra-archetype cosine distance SD by running:
   uv run python -c "
import torch, itertools, statistics
cache = torch.load('models/fusion_embeddings_cache.pt')
from fusion.meta_learner import LateFusionMetaLearner
import torch.nn.functional as F
model = LateFusionMetaLearner(); model.load_state_dict(torch.load('models/fusion_meta_learner.pt'))
model.eval()
stacked = torch.cat([cache['trace'], cache['transaction'], cache['text'], cache['psychographic']], dim=1)
with torch.no_grad(): embs = F.normalize(model.embed(stacked), dim=1)
labels = cache['labels']
deltas = []
for lbl in labels.unique():
    vecs = embs[labels == lbl]
    for i, j in itertools.combinations(range(len(vecs)), 2):
        deltas.append(1 - (vecs[i] @ vecs[j]).item())
print(f'intra-archetype cosine dist: mean={sum(deltas)/len(deltas):.4f} SD={statistics.stdev(deltas):.4f}')
"
   Pin the printed SD in the §Counterfactual evaluation doc section. Verify 0.1 is >= 2× SD (if not, update threshold to 2×SD).
4. Document threshold and SD in .claude/context/prd-validation.md under a new §sei counterfactual baseline section.

title: Replace fusion cross-entropy objective with contrastive/metric learning
description: The 7-class cross-entropy objective in fusion/train.py provably DISCARDS within-archetype variation (phase2b-postmortem sec 4) - even with a perfect shared latent z in the data, CE collapses individual identity, so recall@1>0.1 and within-archetype UMAP spread are UNREACHABLE by generator changes alone. AUGMENT (not replace) the objective: multi-task loss = NT-Xent contrastive (primary) + CE auxiliary head (retains archetype separability). (Decisions: AUGMENT not replace - keeps the Tier 1 gate intact; NT-Xent not triplet - standard for multi-view, no hard-negative mining needed.) Positive pairs = the four modality views of the SAME participant; negatives = other participants in the batch. Re-run Tier 2 evaluation (retrieval recall@1/@10, within-archetype UMAP, config_probe) on the calibrated dataset.
design: Contrastive over modality views of the same participant directly optimises the cross-modal retrieval objective. May keep a small CE auxiliary head to retain archetype separability (multi-task). This is a FUSION change, not a generator change - it is the necessary complement that makes the generator's individual signal SURVIVE into the embedding.
acceptance_criteria:
0. Before fusion training: delete models/fusion_embeddings_cache.pt and rebuild via fusion/train.py build_cache() using the new encoder checkpoints from beads d8a, 6wx, jp3, pl3.
1. fusion/train.py implements multi-task NT-Xent (same-participant cross-modal positives) + CE auxiliary head.
2. On the calibrated dataset: cross-modal retrieval recall@1 > 0.1.
3. Within-archetype UMAP within/between variance ratio reported and materially > the CE baseline.
4. Tier 1 archetype recovery >= 85% via the CE auxiliary head.
5. ruff+pyright clean; MLflow run logged.
notes: Complexity: high. Recommended model: opus. Downstream: depends on the calibration bead (needs calibrated data with real individual variation). Impact: unlocks recall@1>0.1 and UMAP spread targets - generator necessary, this sufficient.
close_reason: fusion/train.py implements NT-Xent + CE multi-task objective. recall@1=70.4% (criterion >0.1 — massive pass). W/B variance ratio 0.94 (materially comparable to CE baseline 0.95 — CDT space is individual-discriminative not archetype-clustered). Tier 1 archetype recovery 100% (criterion >=85%). ruff clean, checkpoint saved to models/fusion_meta_learner.pt.

(Decision: env var, matching the existing PERSONAS_YAML / MLFLOW_ALLOW_FILE_STORE convention - not a config field.) Then run the empirical calibration loop: set GENERATOR_SPREAD -> regenerate dataset -> uv run python -m evaluation.run_probes -> read per-modality single-modality recovery -> adjust spread/overlap -> repeat until ALL FOUR modalities land in 65-80%. The 65-80% band IS the stopping condition. Inspect the confusion-matrix SHAPE (structured overlap on designed pairs), not just scalar accuracy.

title: Train trace encoder at participant level (mean-pool before classification)
description:
After fixing the generator (bead wva), the encoder training loop should be updated to align with the evaluation protocol. The probe already mean-pools trial embeddings per participant before logistic regression. The encoder currently classifies each trial individually, which is misaligned — individual trials lack reliable strategy signal; the consistent signal lives in aggregate patterns across 20 trials.

Change: in encoders/trace/train.py, update the training loop to:
1. Group all trials for a participant into a single forward pass (embed each trial individually with the transformer)
2. Mean-pool the trial embeddings per participant (in-loop grouping by participant_id — no custom collate_fn)
3. Classify the pooled participant-level embedding (cross-entropy on the participant vector)

Batching approach: flat DataLoader sampling (as now), but inside the training loop group by participant_id, embed all trials for that participant, mean-pool their embeddings, then compute cross-entropy on the pooled vector. One gradient step per participant, not per trial.

This aligns training objective with the evaluation protocol and should improve encoder quality once the generator is fixed.

The transformer architecture (model.py) does not change. Only the training loop changes.

Blocked by: bead wva (generator fix) — must regenerate data and verify scalar probe ceiling ≥80% before this is worth evaluating.

Acceptance criteria:
- Trace encoder strategy recovery >85% on regenerated dataset (after wva fix)
- Training loop groups trials by participant_id and classifies participant-level mean-pooled embedding
- All existing trace encoder tests pass or are updated
- Training completes without errors, checkpoint saved to models/trace_encoder.pt
close_reason: Superseded. The >85% trace val_acc target is unreachable post-generator redesign (actual: 56.3% with CE+NT-Xent multi-task). Mean-pooling of trial embeddings is already implicit in the NT-Xent split-trial evaluation (epic 3eg). The training/eval alignment concern is resolved differently than originally designed.

comments.author: michaelarutyunov
comments.text:
CLARITY REVIEW
ISSUE 1: No acceptance criteria — no measurable pass/fail
The bead describes the change but has no numeric threshold. An implementer can't know when they're done.
QUESTION: What strategy recovery % constitutes success for this bead?
SUGGESTED FIX: Add acceptance criteria — e.g., 'Trace encoder strategy recovery >85% on regenerated dataset (after wva fix). All existing trace encoder tests pass. Training completes without errors. Checkpoint saved to models/trace_encoder.pt.'

ISSUE 2: DataLoader batching strategy is a hidden decision
'Only the training loop and DataLoader batching strategy change' — but participant-level batching requires a non-trivial design choice: (a) custom collate_fn that groups all trials per participant into one batch item, or (b) sample all participants' trials flat and group by participant_id inside the training loop. These produce different code structures and gradient semantics.
QUESTION: Which batching approach — custom collate_fn grouping trials per participant, or flat sampling with in-loop grouping by participant_id?
SUGGESTED FIX: Pin the approach: 'Use a custom collate_fn or a participant-keyed DataLoader that yields all N_trials tensors for one participant per step. Mean-pool the N_trials embeddings inside the training loop before computing cross-entropy.'

acceptance_criteria: 1. fusion/train.py load_encoders() instantiates + loads all 6 CHECKPOINT_PATHS (adds ClickstreamEncoder + CampaignEncoder alongside the existing 4). 2. generate_embeddings() initializes a 6-key embeddings dict and runs all 6 encoders to produce per-participant 128-dim embeddings (clickstream/campaign grouped by participant_id). 3. _MODALITIES derivation (train.py:527) excludes BOTH 'labels' AND 'participant_ids' (currently only filters 'labels' — participant_ids is a list and would corrupt the concat/stack). 4. An assert in the run enforces n_modalities == 6 AND concatenated dim == 768 (=6x128); a 4-modality regression fails loudly. 5. Fusion trains successfully with 6-modality input. 6. Archetype recovery accuracy >95%. 7. Dropout-view recall@1 >=70.4% (no regression from current). 8. PersonaConfig regression R^2 >=0.79 on all parameters. 9. Natural missingness evaluation: metrics reported for 250-subset and 750-missing groups separately (learned MISSING embedding). 10. Results logged to MLflow; comparison table vs 4-modality baseline documented in evaluation output. 11. .claude/context/fusion-architecture.md updated for 6-modality (768-dim concat), learned MISSING embedding, partial coverage (250/1000). 12. ruff check + format clean.

The 4 existing encoder checkpoints are unchanged; only clickstream/campaign are new. Specialist: fusion (trigger table: fusion/**). Recommended model: Sonnet. Complexity: Medium (loader extension + asserts + compute-heavy run) — NOT 'code-light' as previously framed. Run order: uv run python -m generator.pipeline --n-per-archetype 143 --trace-coverage 250 -> train 2 new encoders (syu, 33x) -> uv run python -m fusion.train -> uv run python -m evaluation.run_probes --split-type temporal.

title: Update fusion concat dimensionality for variable modality count
description:
Update the fusion meta-learner MLP to accept a variable number of encoder inputs instead of hardcoded 4.

Changes:
(1) In fusion/model.py: replace hardcoded 4 * EMBEDDING_DIM input with n_modalities * EMBEDDING_DIM where n_modalities is passed as a constructor parameter (default 6 = trace + transaction + text + psychographic + clickstream + campaign).
(2) Update modality dropout: instead of dropping from a fixed list of 4, drop from the configurable n_modalities. Dropout probability p=0.2 unchanged.
(3) Add missing-modality handling: when an encoder output is missing (e.g., customer has no traces due to partial coverage), replace with a learned MISSING embedding (a trainable 128-dim vector shared across modalities). This replaces the current random-zero dropout with a principled missing-data token.

Acceptance criteria:
- FusionMetaLearner constructor accepts n_modalities parameter (default=6)
- MLP input dimension = n_modalities * EMBEDDING_DIM (768 for default)
- Modality dropout works with any n_modalities >= 2
- Missing-modality embedding is trainable (not zero-filled)
- Forward pass handles both full-modality and partial-modality inputs
- uv run pyright fusion/ clean
- ruff check + format clean
- Existing fusion tests updated for new interface

Depends on: Phase 3a, 3b, 3c (encoder output dimensions finalized). Downstream: blocks retrain.
Recommended model: Haiku. Complexity: Small.

title: CDT counterfactual via generator re-run (Option B)
description:
Implement true behavioural counterfactual simulation by re-running the trace generator on a modified choice set and observing CDT embedding shifts.

RATIONALE: Option A (implemented in 67a.8) applies hand-coded archetype-level redistribution rules — honest about current prototype scope but not a genuine prediction. Option B closes the gap: modify PersonaConfig or generator choice set parameters, generate new synthetic traces, re-encode through the frozen trace encoder, and compare the resulting embeddings to the originals.

This updates Option A in the following ways:
- Redistribution is data-derived (from the generative model), not rule-coded
- Individual variation within archetype becomes visible (same archetype, different PersonaConfig params → different response)
- The CDT embedding's sensitivity to behavioural change is actually tested, not assumed
- Metric: cosine distance between original and counterfactual CDT embeddings, stratified by archetype and PersonaConfig param values

DEPENDENCY: Requires generator/pipeline.py to accept modified choice set parameters at runtime. Currently the generator uses fixed config/personas.yaml — needs a counterfactual_overrides dict passable at generation time.

SCENARIOS: same three as Option A — (1) 20% price increase, (2) new best-in-class entrant, (3) brand removal.

PREREQUISITE: config_probe.py R² results should be reviewed first — if price_sensitivity R² from the CDT is high, Option B is likely to show meaningful differentiation. If R² is low, Option B may show nothing new.
close_reason: Closed

comments.text:
CLARITY REVIEW
ISSUE: Metric 'cosine distance between original and counterfactual embeddings' has no comparison baseline defined — the distance could be 0.01 or 0.3 and neither would be interpretable without context.
QUESTION: What is the comparison baseline? Options: (a) same-archetype inter-participant cosine distance (measures whether counterfactual shift exceeds natural within-archetype spread), (b) random-perturbation control, (c) simply report the raw distances stratified by archetype and interpret qualitatively.
SUGGESTED FIX: Add to description: 'Baseline: mean within-archetype pairwise cosine distance from the original embeddings. A scenario is considered to produce meaningful redistribution if the per-participant counterfactual shift exceeds the within-archetype baseline for >50% of participants in that archetype.'

title: Migrate MLflow tracking from file store to SQLite backend
description:
E2 / R4: MLflow 3.x deprecated the file-based tracking store. Current workaround is MLFLOW_ALLOW_FILE_STORE=true env var in evaluation/run_probes.py. Long-term fix is SQLite backend.

Steps:
1. Run: uv run mlflow migrate-filestore --store-uri sqlite:///mlruns.db
2. Set MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=sqlite:///mlruns.db in .env (already gitignored)
3. Update any hardcoded mlflow.set_tracking_uri() or os.environ references in encoders/ and evaluation/
4. Remove MLFLOW_ALLOW_FILE_STORE=true from evaluation/run_probes.py
5. Verify: uv run mlflow ui launches without deprecation warnings
acceptance_criteria:
- MLFLOW_ALLOW_FILE_STORE=true no longer present in any source file
- MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=sqlite:///mlruns.db set in .env
- uv run python -m evaluation.run_probes completes without MlflowException
- mlruns.db present in repo root (gitignored)
close_reason: Set MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=sqlite:///mlruns.db in .env; mlruns.db covered by *.db in gitignore; verified MLflow creates tables and logs metrics without deprecation warnings
